quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 8, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Lewis Structures for Covalent Compounds
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Lewis Dot Structures, Covalent Bonding, Octet Rule, Lone Pairs, Multiple Bonds, Expanded Octets

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for water (H₂O)?
options: Oxygen with 8 electrons, two single bonds to hydrogen, and two lone pairs
Oxygen with 6 electrons, two single bonds to hydrogen, and no lone pairs
Oxygen with 8 electrons, two double bonds to hydrogen, and no lone pairs
Oxygen with 10 electrons, two single bonds to hydrogen, and one lone pair
questions.explanation: Water (H₂O) has oxygen (Group 16) with 6 valence electrons. It forms two single bonds with hydrogen atoms (using 2 electrons), leaving two lone pairs (4 electrons). This gives oxygen a full octet (8 electrons total). Each hydrogen has 2 electrons (duet).

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for carbon dioxide (CO₂)?
options: Carbon with 8 electrons, two double bonds to oxygen atoms, and no lone pairs on carbon
Carbon with 6 electrons, two single bonds to oxygen, and two lone pairs
Carbon with 10 electrons, two triple bonds to oxygen atoms
Carbon with 8 electrons, two single bonds to oxygen, and two lone pairs
questions.explanation: CO₂ has carbon (Group 14) with 4 valence electrons. It forms two double bonds with oxygen atoms (sharing 4 electrons with each oxygen). Each oxygen has two lone pairs. Carbon achieves an octet, and each oxygen achieves an octet through the double bonds and lone pairs.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for carbon monoxide (CO)?
options: Carbon and oxygen connected by a triple bond, with one lone pair on carbon and one lone pair on oxygen
Carbon and oxygen connected by a double bond, with two lone pairs on each atom
Carbon and oxygen connected by a single bond, with three lone pairs on oxygen and one on carbon
Carbon and oxygen connected by a triple bond, with one lone pair on carbon and three lone pairs on oxygen
questions.explanation: CO has a triple bond between carbon and oxygen. Carbon has one lone pair, and oxygen has one lone pair. This gives both atoms an octet (carbon: 3 bonds + 1 lone pair = 8 electrons; oxygen: 3 bonds + 1 lone pair = 8 electrons).

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for methane (CH₄)?
options: Carbon with 8 electrons, four single bonds to hydrogen atoms, and no lone pairs
Carbon with 6 electrons, three single bonds to hydrogen, and one lone pair
Carbon with 10 electrons, four double bonds to hydrogen atoms
Carbon with 8 electrons, two double bonds and two single bonds to hydrogen
questions.explanation: Methane (CH₄) has carbon (Group 14) with 4 valence electrons. It forms four single bonds with four hydrogen atoms, using all its electrons for bonding and leaving no lone pairs on carbon. Carbon achieves an octet through the four shared pairs, and each hydrogen achieves a duet.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for ammonia (NH₃)?
options: Nitrogen with 8 electrons, three single bonds to hydrogen, and one lone pair
Nitrogen with 6 electrons, three single bonds to hydrogen, and no lone pairs
Nitrogen with 8 electrons, two single bonds and one double bond to hydrogen
Nitrogen with 10 electrons, three single bonds to hydrogen, and two lone pairs
questions.explanation: Ammonia (NH₃) has nitrogen (Group 15) with 5 valence electrons. It forms three single bonds with hydrogen atoms (using 3 electrons), leaving one lone pair (2 electrons). This gives nitrogen a total of 8 electrons (octet). Each hydrogen has 2 electrons.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for hydrogen chloride (HCl)?
options: Hydrogen and chlorine connected by a single bond, with three lone pairs on chlorine
Hydrogen and chlorine connected by a double bond, with two lone pairs on chlorine
Hydrogen and chlorine connected by a single bond, with four lone pairs on chlorine
Hydrogen and chlorine connected by a single bond, with no lone pairs on chlorine
questions.explanation: HCl has a single covalent bond between hydrogen and chlorine. Chlorine (Group 17) has 7 valence electrons; it shares one with hydrogen, leaving three lone pairs (6 electrons). This gives chlorine a full octet (8 electrons), and hydrogen achieves a duet (2 electrons).

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for sulfur dioxide (SO₂)?
options: Sulfur with 8 electrons, one double bond and one single bond to oxygen, with one lone pair on sulfur
Sulfur with 8 electrons, two double bonds to oxygen, and no lone pairs
Sulfur with 8 electrons, two single bonds to oxygen, and two lone pairs
Sulfur with 12 electrons, two double bonds to oxygen, and one lone pair
questions.explanation: SO₂ has resonance structures. In the octet-rule Lewis structure, sulfur forms one double bond and one single bond with oxygen atoms, with one lone pair on sulfur, giving sulfur an octet (8 electrons). Each oxygen also achieves an octet. (Note: Sulfur can expand its octet in other representations, but this is the standard Grade 11 model.)

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for sulfur trioxide (SO₃)?
options: Sulfur with 12 electrons, three double bonds to oxygen atoms, and no lone pairs on sulfur
Sulfur with 8 electrons, three single bonds to oxygen, and one lone pair
Sulfur with 10 electrons, two double bonds and one single bond to oxygen
Sulfur with 8 electrons, three double bonds to oxygen, and no lone pairs
questions.explanation: SO₃ has sulfur with an expanded octet. In its most common Lewis structure, sulfur forms three double bonds with oxygen atoms, giving sulfur 12 electrons. Sulfur has no lone pairs, and each oxygen has two lone pairs. This structure minimizes formal charges.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for nitrogen dioxide (NO₂)?
options: Nitrogen with 7 electrons, one double bond and one single bond to oxygen, with an unpaired electron on nitrogen
Nitrogen with 8 electrons, two double bonds to oxygen, and no lone pairs
Nitrogen with 10 electrons, two single bonds to oxygen, and two lone pairs
Nitrogen with 8 electrons, one double bond and one single bond to oxygen, with no unpaired electrons
questions.explanation: NO₂ is a free radical with an odd number of electrons. Nitrogen forms one double bond and one single bond with oxygen atoms, leaving one unpaired electron on nitrogen. Nitrogen has 7 electrons around it (incomplete octet), making it a paramagnetic molecule.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for nitrous oxide (N₂O)?
options: Linear structure with N≡N–O resonance, with formal charges on terminal nitrogen and oxygen
Bent structure with N–N–O single bonds and lone pairs on all atoms
Linear structure with N–N≡O and no formal charges
Cyclic structure with all single bonds
questions.explanation: N₂O (nitrous oxide) has a linear structure with resonance. The most stable Lewis structure is N≡N⁺–O⁻, with a triple bond between the first two nitrogens, a single bond to oxygen, and formal charges. Other resonance structures exist, but the molecule is linear.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for nitrogen monoxide (NO)?
options: Nitrogen and oxygen connected by a double bond, with an unpaired electron on nitrogen and three lone pairs on oxygen
Nitrogen and oxygen connected by a triple bond, with no unpaired electrons
Nitrogen and oxygen connected by a single bond, with three lone pairs on each atom
Nitrogen and oxygen connected by a double bond, with all electrons paired
questions.explanation: NO (nitric oxide) is a free radical with 11 valence electrons. It has a double bond between N and O, with an unpaired electron on nitrogen. Oxygen has three lone pairs. The molecule is paramagnetic due to the unpaired electron.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for hydrogen sulfide (H₂S)?
options: Sulfur with 8 electrons, two single bonds to hydrogen, and two lone pairs
Sulfur with 6 electrons, two single bonds to hydrogen, and no lone pairs
Sulfur with 10 electrons, two double bonds to hydrogen, and one lone pair
Sulfur with 8 electrons, one double bond and one single bond to hydrogen
questions.explanation: H₂S is similar to water but with sulfur. Sulfur (Group 16) has 6 valence electrons. It forms two single bonds with hydrogen atoms (using 2 electrons), leaving two lone pairs (4 electrons). Sulfur achieves an octet (8 electrons). Each hydrogen has a duet.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for phosphorus trichloride (PCl₃)?
options: Phosphorus with 8 electrons, three single bonds to chlorine, and one lone pair
Phosphorus with 10 electrons, three single bonds to chlorine, and two lone pairs
Phosphorus with 6 electrons, three double bonds to chlorine, and no lone pairs
Phosphorus with 8 electrons, two single bonds and one double bond to chlorine
questions.explanation: PCl₃ has phosphorus (Group 15) with 5 valence electrons. It forms three single bonds with chlorine atoms (using 3 electrons), leaving one lone pair (2 electrons). This gives phosphorus an octet. Each chlorine forms one bond and has three lone pairs.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for phosphorus pentachloride (PCl₅)?
options: Phosphorus with 10 electrons, five single bonds to chlorine atoms, and no lone pairs
Phosphorus with 8 electrons, five single bonds to chlorine, and expanded octet impossible
Phosphorus with 12 electrons, three double bonds and two single bonds to chlorine
Phosphorus with 8 electrons, four single bonds and one lone pair
questions.explanation: PCl₅ has phosphorus with an expanded octet. Phosphorus forms five single bonds with five chlorine atoms, using all 5 valence electrons and accommodating 10 electrons total. This is possible because phosphorus is in Period 3. Each chlorine has three lone pairs.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Lewis structure for carbon tetrachloride (CCl₄)?
options: Carbon with 8 electrons, four single bonds to chlorine atoms, and no lone pairs
Carbon with 6 electrons, three single bonds to chlorine, and one lone pair
Carbon with 10 electrons, four double bonds to chlorine atoms
Carbon with 8 electrons, two double bonds and two single bonds to chlorine
questions.explanation: CCl₄ has carbon (Group 14) with 4 valence electrons. It forms four single bonds with four chlorine atoms, using all its electrons for bonding and leaving no lone pairs on carbon. Carbon achieves an octet through the four shared pairs. Each chlorine atom has three lone pairs.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered Lewis structures for covalent compounds! 🧪🔗
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review molecules with expanded octets and resonance structures.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on lone pair placement and octet rule exceptions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review Lewis structure rules, including bond formation and electron counting.
